faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Everything about using Video to Prompt as a single-purpose imitation shooting tool.
faq.tip: Need implementation help? Talk to support

items.question: What does Video to Prompt generate?
items.answer: Each run returns a complete package: master prompt, short prompt, negative prompt, keyword tags, and shot list.

items.question: Which input formats are supported?
items.answer: You can upload MP4, WebM, MOV, AVI, and MKV, or provide a public video URL.

items.question: Can I output prompts in Chinese?
items.answer: Yes. Output language follows your current site language (English or Chinese).

items.question: How is credit usage calculated?
items.answer: Credit cost is estimated mainly from video duration and analysis complexity. Estimated cost is shown before you run.

items.question: Can I reuse prompts in other AI tools?
items.answer: Yes. Output is plain text and designed for direct copy into Runway, Kling, Pika, Sora, and internal workflows.

items.question: Is this suitable for teams?
items.answer: Absolutely. Teams use Video to Prompt to keep style language consistent and speed up campaign iteration.

cta.title: Turn Reference Videos into Reusable Shooting Playbooks
cta.description: Upload, decompose, copy, and execute—one focused workflow for reliable reenactment.

metadata.title: Video to Prompt
metadata.description: Turn any video into structured prompts for AI video generation. Extract scene, style, camera language, and shot list in seconds.
metadata.keywords: video to prompt, AI prompt generator, video prompt builder, shot list generator, prompt engineering, AI video workflow
